0.title: IHK Final Examination - Exam Structure
0.snippet:
IHK Final Examination - Exam Structure

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Understand the structure of the extended IHK final examination
- Know the weighting of individual exam components
- Place the exam schedule in context
- Know the five action steps of Part 1

---

   Core Content

    Overview: The Extended Final Examination

With the reorganization of IT professions from August 2020, an   extended IHK final examination   was introduced. The examination

1.title: Exam Catalog 2025 - Changes and Focus Areas
1.snippet:
Exam Catalog 2025 - Changes and Focus Areas

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Know the most important changes in the exam catalog from 2025
- Know which topics have been NEWLY added
- Understand which topics have been REMOVED
- Properly prioritize exam focus areas

---

   Core Content

    Important Notice

Starting January 2025, a   new exam catalog   applies to AP1 for IT professions. This learning material has been updated accordingly and contains   only

2.title: Learning Strategy for AP1
2.snippet:
Learning Strategy for AP1

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Develop an effective learning strategy for AP1
- Know and practice the different task types
- Know how to optimally allocate your study time
- Apply tips for the exam situation

---

   Core Content

    The Right Preparation

AP1 is a   practice-oriented exam  . It's not about memorizing facts, but about   solving complex tasks   related to specific operational situations.

     Basic Principles

1

3.title: Project Management - Overview
3.snippet:
Project Management - Overview

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Understand the fundamentals of project management
- Distinguish projects from routine tasks
- Apply important planning tools (Gantt, network diagram)
- Know and compare process models (Waterfall, Scrum)
- Apply the SMART principle for goal formulation

---

   Core Content

    What is Project Management?

  Project management   encompasses the planning, steering, and controlling of projects. It

4.title: Project Definition
4.snippet:
Project Definition

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Distinguish a project from a routine task
- Know the five characteristics of a project
- Give examples of projects and non-projects

---

   Core Content

    What is a Project?

A   project   is an undertaking with the following characteristic features:

    The 5 Characteristics of a Project

| Characteristic | Explanation |
|----------------|-------------|
|   Uniqueness   | The undertaking is new and n

5.title: Project Phases
5.snippet:
Project Phases

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Know the four main phases of a project
- Describe the tasks in each phase
- Understand the difference between phase model and process model

---

   Core Content

    The Four Project Phases

Every project fundamentally goes through four main phases:

|   1. PROJECT   |   |   2. PROJECT   |   |   3. PROJECT   |   |   4. PROJE

6.title: SMART Principle
6.snippet:
SMART Principle

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Explain the SMART principle
- Know all five letters of the acronym
- Formulate goals according to the SMART principle
- Recognize and improve non-SMART goals

---

   Core Content

    What is the SMART Principle?

The   SMART principle   is a method for formulating clear and concrete goals. SMART is an acronym that stands for five characteristics that a good goal should have.

>   NEW from 2025:   The SMART

7.title: Gantt Chart
7.snippet:
Gantt Chart

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Understand the structure of a Gantt chart
- Read and interpret a Gantt chart
- Recognize the critical path in a Gantt chart
- Create simple Gantt charts yourself

---

   Core Content

    What is a Gantt Chart?

The   Gantt chart   (also bar chart) is a tool for graphically representing project schedules. It shows activities as horizontal bars on a timeline.

  Named after:   Henry Laurence Gantt (1861-1919), Am

8.title: Network Diagram Technique
8.snippet:
Network Diagram Technique

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Understand the structure of a network diagram
- Calculate ES, EF, LS, LF
- Calculate total float and free float
- Determine the critical path

---

   Core Content

    What is a Network Diagram?

The   network diagram   is a graphical method for representing project schedules and their temporal dependencies. It enables the calculation of buffer times and the identification of the critical path.

>

9.title: Waterfall Model
9.snippet:
Waterfall Model

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Explain the waterfall model
- Know the phases of the waterfall model
- Name advantages and disadvantages
- Know when the waterfall model is suitable

---

   Core Content

    What is the Waterfall Model?

The   waterfall model   is a   linear process model   for software development and project management. The phases are completed one after another - like a waterfall that only flows in one direction.

---

10.title: Scrum
10.snippet:
Scrum

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Explain Scrum as an agile framework
- Know and describe the three Scrum roles
- Know the Scrum meetings (events)
- Understand the Scrum artifacts
- Distinguish Scrum from the waterfall model

---

   Core Content

    What is Scrum?

  Scrum   is an   agile framework   for developing and managing products. It enables iterative and incremental development in short cycles (Sprints).

>   Note:   Kanban, XP, and other agi

11.title: Requirements Specification and Functional Specification
11.snippet:
Requirements Specification and Functional Specification

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Explain the difference between requirements specification and functional specification
- Know who creates which document
- Know the typical contents of both documents
- Understand the function of both documents in the project lifecycle

---

   Core Content

    Overview

   
+--------------------+                    +--------------------+
|   REQUIREMENTS     |  ------

12.title: Needs Analysis
12.snippet:
Needs Analysis

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Explain the purpose of a needs analysis
- Know the steps of a needs analysis
- Name methods for needs assessment
- Understand the connection to requirements and functional specifications

---

   Core Content

    What is a Needs Analysis?

A   needs analysis   determines the actual needs and requirements as a basis for decisions and project planning. It answers the question:   "What is really needed?"  

---

13.snippet:
Team Formation Phases (Tuckman Model)

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Know the four team formation phases according to Tuckman
- Describe the characteristics of each phase
- Understand how teams develop
- Understand the role of leadership in the different phases

---

   Core Content

    The Tuckman Model

The   Tuckman Model   describes the development of teams in four phases. It was developed in 1965 by Bruce Tuckman and is the most well-known model for

14.title: Reflection Methods
14.snippet:
Reflection Methods

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Understand the purpose of reflection methods
- Explain Lessons Learned
- Know the importance of a feedback culture
- Know when reflection takes place in a project

---

   Core Content

    Why Reflection?

Reflection serves to learn from experiences and continuously improve. In project management and agile methods, regular reflection is an important component.

  Goals of Reflection:  
- Learn from mistak

15.title: Quality Management - Chapter Overview
15.snippet:
Quality Management - Chapter Overview

   Introduction

Quality Management (QM) is a systematic approach to ensuring and improving the quality of products and services. In the IT industry, QM plays a central role in software development, the provision of IT services, and the setup of workstations.

---

   Exam Relevance

Quality Management is an important component of the AP1 exam. Typical exam topics are:

- Definition and significance of quality
- DIN EN ISO 9000ff. series of standards
- Th

16.title: Quality Concept
16.snippet:
Quality Concept

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter you should be able to:
- Define the term quality
- Distinguish between product and process quality
- Explain the Kano model
- Understand inherent characteristics

---

   Core Content

    What is Quality?

  Quality   refers to the degree to which a set of inherent characteristics of an object fulfills requirements (according to DIN EN ISO 9000).

Put more simply:
> Quality = Degree of fulfillment of requirements

  Inherent characte

17.title: Quality Management Systems (QMS)
17.snippet:
Quality Management Systems (QMS)

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter you should:
- Know what a quality management system is
- Know the components of a QMS
- Be able to describe the steps for implementing a QMS
- Know the difference between QMS models

---

   Core Content

    What is a QMS?

A   Quality Management System (QMS)   is a structured approach to ensuring and continuously improving quality in organizations. It encompasses:

-   Processes:   Defined workflows
-   Policies:

18.title: DIN EN ISO 9000ff.

DIN EN ISO 9000ff.

After this chapter you should be able to:
- Explain the DIN EN ISO 9000 series of standards
- Know the abbreviations DIN, EN, ISO, IEC

- Understand the difference between ISO 9000, 9001 and 9004

19.title: Certification
19.snippet:
Certification

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter you should:
- Understand the purpose of certification
- Be able to describe the certification process
- Know what an audit is
- Know various certification examples

---

   Core Content

    What is Certification?

  Certification   is formal proof that an organization, a product, or a person meets established standards. It is carried out by independent, external testing bodies.

  Benefits of certification:  
- Proof of quality capabil

20.title: Total Quality Management (TQM)
20.snippet:
Total Quality Management (TQM)

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter you should be able to:
- Explain TQM as a management philosophy
- Know the core principles of TQM
- Describe the EFQM model
- Distinguish TQM from ISO 9001

---

   Core Content

    What is TQM?

  Total Quality Management (TQM)   is a comprehensive management philosophy that was developed in the USA in the 1940s and successfully implemented in Japan in the 1950s.

  The three components of the name:  

| Term | Meanin

21.title: PDCA Cycle
21.snippet:
PDCA Cycle

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter you should be able to:
- Explain the PDCA cycle
- Describe the four phases
- Understand the application of the PDCA cycle
- Know the connection to continuous improvement

---

   Core Content

    What is the PDCA Cycle?

The   PDCA cycle   (also called Deming cycle or Deming circle) is an iterative method for continuously improving processes, products, and services.

  PDCA stands for:  
-   P  lan
-   D  o
-   C  heck
-   A  ct

---

22.title: Software Quality
22.snippet:
Software Quality

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter you should:
- Know the quality characteristics of software
- Understand methods for quality assurance in software development
- Be able to create test protocols
- Understand the connection between software quality and QM

---

   Core Content

    What is Software Quality?

  Software quality   refers to the degree to which a software product fulfills specified and implied requirements. It encompasses both functional and non-function

23.title: Accessibility in IT
23.snippet:
Accessibility in IT

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter you should be able to:
- Define accessibility in the IT sector
- Know the legal foundations (EU Directive, BITV 2.0)
- Describe measures for accessible IT
- Know the WCAG guidelines

---

   Core Content

    What is Accessibility?

  Accessibility   means that IT systems, websites, and applications are designed so that they can be used by   all people   - regardless of physical or mental limitations.

>   Definition:   Accessible

24.title: Data Protection - Chapter Overview
24.snippet:
Data Protection - Chapter Overview

   Introduction

Data protection refers to the protection against misuse of personal data and protects the right to informational self-determination. In the digital world, data protection is particularly important because data can be easily collected, processed, and shared.

---

   Exam Relevance

Data protection is an important topic in the AP1 exam. Particularly relevant are:

- GDPR principles and fundamentals
- Personal data (definition and examples)
-

25.title: GDPR Principles
25.snippet:
GDPR Principles

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Classify the GDPR as an EU-wide regulation
- Know the 6 principles of data processing
- Name the legal bases for data processing
- Understand the relationship between GDPR and BDSG

---

   Core Content

    What is the GDPR?

The   General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)   is an EU regulation that has been in force since May 25, 2018. It uniformly regulates the protection of personal data throughout the EU

26.title: Personal Data
26.snippet:
Personal Data

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Define personal data
- Provide examples of personal data
- Know special categories of personal data
- Distinguish what is personal and what is not

---

   Core Content

    Definition

  Personal data   according to Art. 4(1) GDPR is any information relating to an   identified or identifiable natural person  .

> A person is   identifiable   if they can be identified directly or indirectly, e.g., by:
> - Name

27.title: Data Subject Rights
27.snippet:
Data Subject Rights

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Know the most important data subject rights under GDPR
- Explain the individual rights
- Know how data subjects can exercise their rights
- Know the consequences of violations

---

   Core Content

    What are Data Subject Rights?

The GDPR grants   data subjects   comprehensive rights to ensure control over their personal data. These rights are regulated in   Articles 12-22 GDPR  .

The   CIA triad   describes the three fundamental protection goals of information security. The name is derived from the English

Security by Design and Security by Default

45.title: Digital Certificates
45.snippet:
Digital Certificates

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Explain the purpose of digital certificates
- Know the components of a certificate
- Understand the role of Certificate Authorities (CAs)
- Describe the trust model (PKI)

---

   Core Content

    What is a Digital Certificate?

A   digital certificate   is an electronic document that links the identity of a person or organization with a public key. It is issued by a trusted authority (CA).

   
+------

46.title: Digital Signatures
46.snippet:
Digital Signatures

   Learning Objectives

After this chapter, you should be able to:
- Explain the purpose of digital signatures
- Understand the process of signature creation and verification
- Know the difference from encryption
- Name application areas

---

   Core Content

    What is a Digital Signature?

A   digital signature   is a cryptographic method that ensures the   authenticity   and   integrity   of digital data (e.g., documents, emails).

   
+--------------------------------

- Master number systems and their conversion

57.snippet:
UPS (Uninterruptible Power Supply)

   Task 1: Project Management (18 Points)

    Task 1.1 (4 Points)

  Four typical project characteristics:  

1.   Uniqueness   - The migration to CloudTravel Pro is a one-time undertaking
2.   Time limitation   - Defined start and end date
3.   Defined goal   - Introduction of the new booking software
4.   Limited resources   - Budget, personnel, time are limited

   Task 1: Industry 4.0 and AI (20 Points)

    Task 1.1 (6 Points)

  a) Industry 4.0 and three characteristics (4 Points):  

  Definition:   Industry 4.0 refers to the fourth industrial revolution, in which production facilities, products, and people are connected via the Internet of Things (IoT) and data is exchanged in real time.

  Three characteristic features:  
1.   Networking   - Machines, systems, and people
